channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Arnott’s Launches Australian Biscuit Range in the UK
item.description: Arnott’s, Australia’s iconic biscuit manufacturer, is launching its Barbecue, Pizza, and Chicken Crimpy Shapes range in the United Kingdom to capitalize on the picnic season and the popularity of tele...

item.title: TMC faces internal crisis as senior minister resigns and aligns with rebel faction
item.description: Former West Bengal Finance Minister Chandrima Bhattacharya, a close ally of Trinamool Congress (TMC) leader Mamata Banerjee, has resigned from all party positions following months of internal strife w...

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Celebrate Wedding at Madison Square Garden
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce celebrated their wedding at Madison Square Garden in New York City, officiated by comedian Adam Sandler. The event featured high-profile guests, elaborate security measur...

item.title: B.C. Lions secure first win of the season with 36-24 victory over Edmonton Elks
item.description: The B.C. Lions claimed their first win of the CFL season with a 36-24 victory over the previously undefeated Edmonton Elks in a high-scoring game at the Apple Bowl. James Butler's 135-yard rushing per...

item.title: Jackie Apostel shares her spinal surgery scar two years after full spinal fusion
item.description: Jackie Apostel, the 30-year-old Brazilian songwriter and girlfriend of Cruz Beckham, has publicly shared her experience with scoliosis surgery by posting a topless image on Instagram showing the scar ...

item.title: JAAC Protests Subside with No Long Marches, Administrative Actions Taken
item.description: Government officials have dismissed rumors of long marches by JAAC protesters, stating that demonstrations will remain localized. The main sit-in at Eidgah continues, with smaller gatherings in Mutyal...

item.title: Alex Eala Advances to Wimbledon Round of 16 with Victory Over Swiatek
item.description: Philippine tennis star Alexandra Eala made history by defeating world No. 1 Iga Swiatek in the third round of Wimbledon 2026, securing her place in the round of 16. The 21-year-old's 7-6 (9), 6-2 vict...

item.title: Brooklyn Beckham and Taylor Swift's wedding: A celebrity milestone
item.description: The article discusses the highly publicized wedding of Brooklyn Beckham, son of David and Victoria Beckham, to Taylor Swift, the renowned pop singer. The event, held at Madison Square Garden, attracte...

item.title: Insights into Trump’s Second Term and Its Impact on Investors
item.description: The article discusses the newly released book 'Regime Change' by journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, which provides an in-depth look at the inner workings of Donald Trump's administration d...

item.title: Farage Faces Parliamentary Probe Over Unreported Benefits from Ally
item.description: Nigel Farage's team has denied allegations that the Reform UK leader breached parliamentary rules by failing to declare benefits received from an ally, George Cottrell. The Sunday Times reported that ...

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader's Funeral: Mourners and Family Pay Tribute
item.description: The article details the funeral procession of late Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, highlighting the somber atmosphere in Tehran as his sons, Mostafa, Meysam, and Masoud Khamenei, along with other...

item.title: Two Arrested After Confrontation Near Victoria’s Beacon Hill Park Leaves Three Injured
item.description: Two individuals have been arrested following a confrontation near Beacon Hill Park in Victoria that resulted in three people sustaining non-life-threatening injuries. Victoria police responded to the ...

item.title: Kenyan Man Missing After 2025 Family Visit: US Police Use Billboard to Reignite Search
item.description: Kenyan national Reuben Waithaka vanished over a year ago while visiting family in the US, and police have now erected a billboard in Alabama to generate fresh leads. The billboard, part of a renewed e...

item.title: Nigeria's Govt. Launches N137bn Road Projects in Borno to Boost Economy and Security
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has launched a N137 billion road rehabilitation project in Borno State, Nigeria, focusing on the Bama–Banki and Dikwa–Gamboru–Ngala roads. The initiative, part of efforts to rest...

item.title: Marcos criticizes China's sanctions on Teodoro, calls for de-escalation in South China Sea tensions
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. criticized China's decision to sanction Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr., calling it 'unhelpful' as it escalates tensions between the Philippines and China. During...

item.title: Advocate against human trafficking and musician Mikayla Simeral sings national anthem at Big Bay Boom
item.description: Mikayla Simeral, a San Diego-based worship leader and social work advocate, recently performed the national anthem at the Big Bay Boom event, blending her dual passions for music and activism. With a ...

item.title: Kimi Antonelli claims British Grand Prix pole position despite Ferrari's strong showing
item.description: Formula One leader Kimi Antonelli secured pole position for the British Grand Prix after outpacing Mercedes teammate George Russell and Ferrari's Charles Leclerc. The 19-year-old Italian, who already ...

item.title: Minister urges continued economic activity and security amid public safety warnings
item.description: Minister for Home Affairs Patrobas Katambi has called on Tanzanians to maintain normal daily operations on July 7, 2026, emphasizing that government measures ensure public safety. During a meeting wit...

item.title: Canadian and U.S. Air Forces Conduct Joint Flypast in Ottawa Ahead of F-35 Jet Delivery
item.description: The Royal Canadian Air Force and the U.S. Air Force conducted a joint flypast over Ottawa to commemorate the 250th anniversary of the United States' Declaration of Independence. The event, held at the...

item.title: Senate Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Begins
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte commenced on July 6, 2026, with the Senate convening as an impeachment court. Senators act as trial judges, overseeing proceedings that will span t...
